pubmed_links: [Miura et al. (2018) Increased serum levels and promoter polymorphisms of macrophage migration inhibitory factor in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29305329/); [Jarboui et al. (2022) Polymorphisms in the hypoxia inducible factor binding site of the MIF gene promoter in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35324982/); [Feng et al. (2021) Multifaceted interconnections between macrophage migration inhibitory factor and psychiatric disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34358623/)
summary: MIF (macrophage migration inhibitory factor) is a pro-inflammatory cytokine whose serum levels are elevated in schizophrenia and depression; promoter polymorphisms in the MIF gene are associated with schizophrenia risk, positioning it as both a biomarker and pathophysiological contributor to psychiatric disorders. (emerging evidence)
